defs: A passage by which an inclosed place may be entered; a place of ingress; entrance;
a narrow waterway leading into a harbor.
quotes: Doors and windows, inlets of men and of light.

defs: A bay or recess, as in the shore of a sea, lake, or large river; a narrow strip of water running into the land or between islands.
That which is let in or inlaid; an inserted material.
notes: ☞ Inlet is also used adjectively, as in inlet pipe, inlet valve, etc.
